introduction: Average (arithmetic mean) is the sum of values divided by the count. It appears in problems about class marks, speeds, ages, and mixtures where each group has a different size.
explanation:
CONCEPT EXPLANATION

Average = Sum of all values / Number of values.

If n numbers have average A, total sum = nA.

When one value x is replaced by y, new average = old average + (y − x)/n.

When a group of average A with count n is merged with average B and count m: combined average = (nA + mB)/(n + m).

Weighted average = Σ(wᵢxᵢ)/Σwᵢ.

REAL-LIFE APPLICATIONS

Grade calculation, cricket batting averages, rainfall per day, portfolio returns, and estimating per-capita statistics.

COMMON MISTAKES

1) Forgetting to update the count when adding/removing items.
2) Averaging averages without weighting by group sizes.
3) Arithmetic errors on total sum.
4) Confusing average speed with average of speeds (need total distance/total time).
5) Not using weighted average for unequal groups.

IMPORTANT NOTES

• Always find total sum first when the average changes.
• Average speed = total distance ÷ total time, not mean of speeds.
• Adding a value equal to the current average leaves the average unchanged.
• Removing the average itself from a set of n items gives new average for (n−1) items only if that value equaled the mean.

shortcutTips.title: Sum first
shortcutTips.tip: Convert average to total sum before adding or removing values.

shortcutTips.title: Equal average add
shortcutTips.tip: Adding a number equal to the average does not change the average.

shortcutTips.title: Replacement delta
shortcutTips.tip: Replacing x by y shifts average by (y−x)/n.

shortcutTips.title: Don't average averages
shortcutTips.tip: Combine using total sums unless groups are equal size.

shortcutTips.title: Speed trap
shortcutTips.tip: Average speed needs total distance and total time.

shortcutTips.title: Weighted groups
shortcutTips.tip: Multiply each group average by its count before combining.

solvedExamples.problem: Average of 5 numbers is 20. One number 15 is replaced by 25. New average?
Class A: 30 students avg 60. Class B: 20 students avg 70. Combined average?
Average age of 8 persons is 24. One 32-year-old leaves. New average?
